markdown.markdown:
```{=html}
Mixed Methods · Public Policy · Arts & Culture

Haedodam Kim

Researcher who tells stories with data

I combine interviews, statistical analysis in R, and data visualization to produce findings that get used — with a focus on the financial, labor, and policy conditions that support long-term sustainability in the arts sector.

Resume
R · tidyverse · fixest Panel Regression Fixed Effects Qualitative Interviews IRB Compliance Data Visualization Tableau Git · GitHub Policy Briefs Multi-stakeholder Coordination Workstream Management Microsoft Office Google Workspace Korean · English

Education

M.S. Civic Analytics

University of Illinois Chicago

Chicago, IL · 2026

Laurette Kirstein Scholarship
Chicago Consular Corps Grant

B.A. Theatre

National Institute for Lifelong Education

South Korea · 2021

A.F.A. Theatre

Seoul Institute of the Arts

South Korea · 2019

Work Experience

Graduate Research Assistant

Office of Social Science Research (OSSR), University of Illinois Chicago

Aug 2025 – May 2026

  • Led 8 cultural and educational programs engaging 160+ participants through a federally funded initiative.
  • Standardized participant surveys across all programs to support program evaluation and federal reporting.
  • Established a monthly newsletter reaching 350+ subscribers, expanding engagement beyond the university community.
  • Expanded university and community partnerships, increasing participation and supporting 2 joint cultural events.

Research Assistant

College of Engineering, University of Illinois Chicago

Jan 2025 – Aug 2025

  • Managed longitudinal biometric and survey data collection for a NIOSH-funded study, maintaining IRB compliance and data quality.
  • Recruited 15 Korean American home care workers and maintained 90%+ retention through bilingual participant support.

Researcher & Project Manager

Korea National University of Arts

Jun 2021 – Dec 2022

  • Conducted interviews with 6 theatre organizations to assess shared storage solutions for environmental sustainability.
  • Produced a 141-page policy report that informed infrastructure planning by the Seoul Metropolitan Government.
  • Promoted to Project Manager to oversee a $1M city-funded sustainability initiative.
  • Coordinated stakeholders across government, academia, and the arts sector to support project implementation.

Selected Projects

01
Featured  AAAE 47th Annual Conference · May 2026

Organizational Capacity as a Policy Intervention Point: Financial Sustainability in U.S. Nonprofit Theater Organizations

What began as a Chicago-focused independent study grew into a national analysis — starting with in-depth interviews with 5 local theater organizations, then expanding to quantitative analysis of 457 U.S. nonprofits to examine how revenue structures and organizational capacity shape financial sustainability.

Mixed Methods Research Regression · In-Depth Interviews Nonprofit Finance · Arts Policy R
→
02
Team Project

Student Enrollment Patterns in a Public Affairs Graduate Program: A 10-Year Trend Analysis

Analysis of 10 years of student enrollment records across Department of Public Policy, Management, and Analytics's MPA, MPP, and MSCA programs — examining course sequencing, course load, time to degree, and shifting subject preferences.

Capstone Project Descriptive Statistics · Trend Analysis Higher Ed · Program Evaluation R · Tableau · Quarto
→
03
Team Project 50th Annual STP&A Conference · October 2026

When More Funding Is Not Enough: Revenue Concentration and Labor Investment in Nonprofit Performing Arts Organizations

Panel data analysis of 4,131 organization-year observations examining how revenue amount and concentration affect labor investment in nonprofit performing arts organizations — using SMU DataArts national dataset (2019–2024).

Academic Paper Fixed Effects · HHI Analysis Nonprofit Labor · Finance · Arts Policy R · fixest · Quarto
→
04

Arts Education Equity in Chicago: A Data Analysis of Arts Access in Public Schools

ZIP code-level analysis of Creative Schools Certification (CSC) scores across Chicago, combining ACS data, spatial mapping, regression, an interactive Shiny dashboard, and a decision tree classifier to examine how socioeconomic conditions shape arts education access.

Data Analysis Report Regression · Spatial Analysis Arts Education · Equity R · Shiny · Quarto · GitHub
→
05

A Policy Memo: Reducing Geographic Disparities in Trauma Care Access in Cook County

A policy memo diagnosing geographic disparities in trauma care access in Cook County through a five-step sequential model and evaluating four policy alternatives using a weighted decision matrix.

Policy Memo Decision Matrix · BOTEC Public Health
→

Contact

(312) 771-1483
·
kimhaedodam@gmail.com
·
haedodamkim
·
Chicago, IL
```
